categories.name: Define

attachments.name: basic_problem_statement.jpg
attachments.description: Basic problem statement focusing on inventory turnover without comprehensive business case

categories.description: Define phase with adequate problem identification but lacking comprehensive Six Sigma project structure and detailed business case development.

statements.name: Problem identified: Low inventory turnover at 4.2x annually vs industry standard 6x
Business case partially developed with estimated impact but incomplete financial analysis
Project scope defined but boundaries not clearly established or communicated
Customer requirements gathered informally without structured Voice of Customer analysis
Project team assembled but roles and responsibilities not clearly defined
Project charter drafted but lacks executive sponsorship and resource commitment

categories.name: Measure

attachments.name: inventory_data_collection.jpg
attachments.description: Manual data collection process without standardized measurement system validation

categories.description: Measure phase with basic data collection but lacking rigorous measurement system analysis and statistical validation.

statements.name: Baseline data collected: Current inventory turnover 4.2x with high variation
Measurement system analysis not performed - data accuracy and repeatability unknown
Basic process capability assessment completed but lacks statistical rigor
Data collection plan informal with inconsistent measurement procedures
Defect definition unclear and not aligned with customer requirements
Sigma level not calculated due to insufficient defect data classification

categories.name: Analyze

attachments.name: basic_cause_analysis.jpg
attachments.description: Simple brainstorming session without advanced statistical analysis or hypothesis testing

categories.description: Analyze phase using basic problem-solving tools but lacking advanced Six Sigma statistical analysis and hypothesis testing.

statements.name: Root cause analysis attempted using simple brainstorming without statistical validation
Basic Pareto analysis completed but lacks depth and statistical significance testing
Process mapping completed but missing detailed flow analysis and value stream assessment
No statistical analysis performed - no DOE, regression, or hypothesis testing
Correlation analysis attempted but lacks statistical rigor and confidence intervals
Key variables identified through observation rather than statistical validation

categories.name: Improve

attachments.name: solution_brainstorming.jpg
attachments.description: Team brainstorming session for improvement solutions without systematic evaluation

categories.description: Improve phase with solution implementation but lacking pilot testing, risk assessment, and systematic validation.

statements.name: Solutions generated through brainstorming but not systematically evaluated
No pilot testing performed - solutions implemented without validation
Risk assessment minimal with no formal mitigation strategies developed
Implementation plan basic without detailed timeline or responsibility matrix
Change management limited - minimal stakeholder engagement or training
Results mixed: 15% improvement achieved but below Six Sigma expectations

categories.name: Control

attachments.name: basic_monitoring_chart.jpg
attachments.description: Simple tracking chart without statistical process control implementation

categories.description: Control phase with basic monitoring but lacking statistical process control and systematic sustainment planning.

statements.name: Basic monitoring established but no statistical process control charts implemented
Process capability not reassessed after improvement implementation
Procedures updated informally without systematic documentation or training
Training provided but not comprehensive or standardized across the organization
No formal response plan for performance degradation or out-of-control situations
Sustainment uncertain - only 2 months of performance tracking completed

highlightsData.title: (Prepare)
highlightsData.description:
Basic Project Setup: Green Belt initiated project with informal preparation lacking comprehensive project charter and executive sponsorship. Initial business case estimation attempted but incomplete financial analysis and resource commitment unclear.

Team Formation Gaps: Project team assembled but roles and responsibilities not clearly defined. Limited Six Sigma expertise available requiring mentorship and additional training support.

highlightsData.title: Define
highlightsData.description:
Problem Identification Adequate: Problem identified as low inventory turnover at 4.2x annually versus industry standard 6x. Project scope defined but boundaries not clearly established or communicated to all stakeholders.

VOC Analysis Missing: Customer requirements gathered informally without structured Voice of Customer analysis. Business case partially developed with estimated impact but lacking comprehensive financial validation.

highlightsData.title: Measure
highlightsData.description:
Basic Data Collection: Baseline data collected showing current inventory turnover 4.2x with high variation. However, measurement system analysis not performed leaving data accuracy and repeatability unknown.

Statistical Rigor Missing: Basic process capability assessment completed but lacks statistical validation. Data collection plan informal with inconsistent measurement procedures and no sigma level calculation performed.

highlightsData.title: Analyze
highlightsData.description:
Limited Analysis Tools: Root cause analysis attempted using simple brainstorming without statistical validation. Basic Pareto analysis completed but lacks depth and statistical significance testing.

Statistical Gap: No advanced statistical analysis performed - no DOE, regression, or hypothesis testing. Key variables identified through observation rather than statistical validation, limiting confidence in findings.

highlightsData.title: Improve
highlightsData.description:
Solution Implementation: Solutions generated through brainstorming but not systematically evaluated. No pilot testing performed with solutions implemented without validation, creating implementation risk.

Mixed Results: 15% improvement achieved but below Six Sigma expectations. Risk assessment minimal with no formal mitigation strategies and limited change management stakeholder engagement.

highlightsData.title: Control
highlightsData.description:
Basic Monitoring Only: Basic monitoring established but no statistical process control charts implemented. Process capability not reassessed after improvement implementation leaving sustainment uncertain.

Sustainment Risk: Procedures updated informally without systematic documentation. Only 2 months of performance tracking completed with no formal response plan for performance degradation situations.

actionItems.title: Enhance Measurement System Analysis and Statistical Validation
actionItems.description: Implement comprehensive MSA study to validate data accuracy and repeatability. Add statistical significance testing and process capability assessment using proper Six Sigma methodology. Engage Black Belt mentor for technical guidance.

attachments.name: msa_implementation_plan.jpg
attachments.description: Measurement System Analysis implementation plan for enhanced data validation

actionItems.title: Implement Statistical Process Control for Sustained Performance
actionItems.description: Deploy proper SPC charts (X-bar and R charts) for inventory turnover monitoring. Establish control limits, response procedures, and systematic review cycles. Train team on SPC interpretation and reaction protocols.

actionItems.title: Conduct Pilot Testing for Additional Improvement Opportunities
actionItems.description: Design and execute pilot tests for remaining improvement solutions. Include statistical validation, risk assessment, and cost-benefit analysis. Use DOE methodology for multi-variable optimization.

actionItems.title: Develop Comprehensive Project Charter and Business Case
actionItems.description: Retrofit project with formal charter including executive sponsorship, resource commitment, and quantified business case. Establish clear project scope, timeline, and success criteria for future DMAIC projects.
